word_list.word: fate leads the willing and drags (along|) the unwilling
word_list.pronunciation: fˈeɪt lˈiːdz ðə wˈɪlɪŋ ænd dɹˈæɡz ɐlˈɑːŋ ɔːɹ ðɪ ʌnwˈɪlɪŋ

senses.definition: used to imply that while fate may guide people toward their destined path, their willingness to cooperate with their circumstances and take action can greatly impact the outcomes they experience

examples.x: When the company offered him a promotion that required him to relocate, he was hesitant at first, but he remembered that fate leads the willing and drags along the unwilling, so he accepted the offer.

word_list.word: if it is meant to be, it will be
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf ɪt ɪz mˈɛnt tə bˈiː ɪt wɪl bˈiː

senses.definition: used to imply that some things are beyond human control and that events will unfold according to a predetermined plan or fate

word_list.word: if you are born to be hanged, then you will never be drowned
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː ɑːɹ bˈɔːɹn təbi hˈæŋd ðˈɛn juː wɪl nˈɛvɚ biː dɹˈaʊnd

senses.definition: used to emphasize that a person's fate is predetermined and unchangeable, and that they will inevitably experience the fate that has been determined for them
used to suggest that while people can make plans and decisions, ultimately the outcome of events is determined by fate or a higher power
used to imply that despite one's careful planning and preparations, unforeseen circumstances or events may disrupt or alter one's intended outcomes

examples.x: The businessman had a detailed strategy for the launch of his new product, but unexpected market changes disrupted his plans, reminding him of the saying 'you plan and God laughs.'

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi bˈʊlɪt hɐz ɐ ɪts bˈɪlɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that destiny or fate determines who shall be killed, rather than chance or random occurrence
